Community Assistance and Crisis Support Resource Guide

Table of Contents

  • Starting Points
  • Rental Assistance Housing and Tenants Rights
  • Food Security and Groceries
  • Trans and Queer Focused Resources
  • Medical Care
  • Employment Assistance
  • Local Neighborhood Organizations and Mutual Aid Groups

This is a list of organizations that can help with issues relating to housing, medical access, and food security. Most of the resources listed here offer many more services than mentioned in this document, and at a minimum can help direct to other organizations that can better offer support that they might not provide.

Starting Points

Odds are that if you need something one of these will help you find it. If they can’t, or you’d rather contact organizations directly, there should still be decent options listed in other sections of this doc.

If you are struggling or overwhelmed, call 211 for help accessing

Calling 211

24/7/365 phone line (operated by United Way) that can connect and refer to resources for non-emergency social services or personal needs. Examples are food assistance, housing, substance use, childcare, clothes, employment, and healthcare. Note that 211 does not offer most services directly, rather they can point you to where to find them. For city services and information directly, call 311.

Website: https://211metrochicago.org/

Chicago Public Library Branches

Other than library services, branch locations operate as physical community resource hubs that you can walk into without knowing what you need and at least get oriented. Some have an on-site social worker, offer job application/resume help, and can just be a safe warm/cool daytime space to be in. A few locations even have free public health vending machines that carry menstrual products, hygiene kits, socks, underwear, Narcan, drug test strips, etc. They can also help navigate 211 and 311 resources. Call individual branch locations to confirm resources, or just walk into any and ask the front desk.

Website: https://chipublib.bibliocommons.com/v2/locations/

Calling 311

24/7/365 phone line that provides information and access to non-emergency services run by the City of Chicago. Unlike 211, this is a direct line to city services ranging from pothole repairs and lead plumbing testing/replacement to landlord issues (illegal lockouts, housing violations) and emergency housing/utility assistance. It can also offer information such as when the next bus will arrive or where the nearest extreme weather shelter is for emergency heating/cooling.

Chicago Department of Family & Support Services

Working with community partners and city/county run programs, the Chicago Department of Family and Support Services connects Chicago residents and families to resources that build stability and support their well-being. They have case managers to help coordinate housing, employment, and medical care together, rather than just connecting to other services to navigate alone (like 211/311). Essentially, the DFSS provides free access to social workers who work for the city.

Website: https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/fss.html

Address: 1615 W. Chicago Ave, 5th Floor

Rental Assistance, Housing, and Tenant’s Rights

These resources can help with evictions, housing violations, heating/cooling bills, landlord disputes, legal aid, emergency housing, and financial assistance.

Calling 311

Already listed, but an operator can help connect you to emergency housing, financial assistance, and information about other options available through the City of Chicago. 311 also supports housing violations, illegal lockouts, and more.

Metropolitan Tenants Org (Eviction Help, Housing Violations, Advocacy)

Offers multiple resources to assert tenant’s rights to assist with evictions, landlord disputes, housing violations, legal aid, and community organizing. They have a hotline you can call for quick assistance.

Website: https://www.tenants-rights.org/

Call: 773-292-4988 (open M-F, 1:00-5:00 pm).

Office: 601 S California (check schedule for walk-in appointments)

Chicago Housing Initiative (Eviction Help, Legal Aid, and More Resources)

The Chicago Housing Initiative helps connect tto groups that offer help with emergency housing, finding low-income housing, cooling/heating assistance, as well as legal support. Website: https://www.chicagohousinginitiative.org/

All Chicago (Housing Security)

All Chicago is a non-profit that provides resources and solutions for housing security, such as emergency financial assistance.

Website: https://allchicago.org/

Food Security and Groceries

These resources can help with food access, including free grocery delivery, hot meals, and supplemental items such as diapers and hygiene products.

Greater Chicago Food Depository (Food Banks)

They are the largest food bank in Chicago, with multiple locations around the city. They also have food assistance programs and can assist with SNAP/Link, Medicaid, and other benefits.

Website: https://www.chicagosfoodbank.org/

Community Kitchen Chicago (Master List of Local Organizations, Free Meals)

They support local food systems and feed people meals free of charge from one of their locations. Additionally, they have a giant list of resources and organizations for all things food related. Check for neighborhood groups providing free grocery delivery, food pantries, hot food programs, and more.

Website: https://www.communitykitchenchicago.org/

Big Resource List!!!: https://www.communitykitchenchicago.org/mutual-aid-map

Love Fridge (Community Fridges and Pantries)

17 free, walk-up access locations stocked with fresh food, dry goods, and household items. Most locations are a public fridge and pantry maintained by community volunteers, neighborhood donors, and local businesses. Check website for a map of fridge locations.

Website: https://www.thelovefridge.com/

Trans and Queer Focused Resources

These are organizations that work with the queer and trans community offering services like gender affirming care, PrEP, and sexual/mental health as well as resources such as food access, support groups, and employment assistance.

Howard Brown Broadway Youth Center (Medical, Mental Health, Dental, Social Services)

Offered through Howard Brown Health, the Broadway Youth Center provides medical, social, and mental wellness services to young people aged 12 to 24 regardless of ability to pay. They also have drop-in services like showers and laundry for people experiencing homelessness or housing instability. Located in Uptown on the North Side of the city. Website: https://howardbrown.org/clinic_location/broadway-youth-center/

Phone: 773.388.1600

Address: 1023 W. Irving Park Road

Brave Space Alliance (Medical, Mental Health, Housing, Social Services)

BSA is a Black trans-led LGBTQ+ Center providing services, programs, and an affirming space where trans and LGBQ+ people are centered, and all are welcome and served. They operate a clinic offering free STD testing along with medical referrals for a variety of care, walk-in appointments with social workers, community pantry, support groups, and temporary housing initiatives. Located in Hyde Park on the South Side of the city.

Website: https://www.bravespacealliance.org/

Address: 1515 E. 52nd Place, 3rd Floor

Planned Parenthood (Sexual/Reproductive Health, Gender Affirming Care, PrEP)

Planned Parenthood is a national organization often thought of as abortion clinics, but locations offer more comprehensive healthcare. This includes STD/HIV testing and prevention, gender affirming care, birth control, miscarriage management, and preventative services like pap tests, breast exams, and urinary tract health. There are multiple locations across the city, with an included link to make an appointment online.

Website: https://www.plannedparenthood.org/planned-parenthood-illinois

Appointments: https://www.plannedparenthood.org/planned-parenthood-illinois/get-care

Locations

  • West Side: 5937 W. Chicago
  • Near North: 1200 N. LaSalle
  • Far North: 5725 N. Broadway
  • Loop/Downtown: 16 N. Wabash,5th Fl.
  • South Side: 11250 S. Halsted

AHF Clinics (Primary Care, STD/HIV Testing and Treatment, PrEP, Pharmacy Services)

AHF Healthcare Centers provide primary care services designed primarily for HIV-positive clients, but being HIV-positive is not a requirement to receive care. They also offer standard health services like pharmaceutical and primary care.

Appointments: https://hivcare.org/ahf-services/

Locations

  • North Side: 3325 N. Halsted St.
  • Near South Side: 2600 S. Michigan Ave.
  • Southside: 1515 East. 52nd Place

Center on Halsted (Behavioral/Sexual Health, Job Assistance, Community Center)

Center on Halsted is a comprehensive community center dedicated to the health and well-being of LGBTQ people in Chicagoland offering such things as employment services, mental health, social programs, and community events. This includes mental health support with therapy, gender-affirming care letters, trauma services, support groups, and assistance for violence survivors. They also have a training and job placement program for those looking to find work in the culinary field as well as training to improve tech skills.

Website: https://www.centeronhalsted.org/

Locations

  • Main: 3656 N Halsted Street
  • Wrigleyville: 806 W Addison St
  • South Side: 6323 S. Cottage Grove Avenue

Medical Care

These are resources to assist with medical care access and affordability. Some Chicago Public Library branches have free vending machines with menstrual products and general hygiene supplies.

CareLink through Cook County Health (Financial Assistance)

CareLink is a free program that helps Cook County residents cover or discount the cost of most services at any Cook County Health facility.

Website: https://cookcountyhealth.org/carelink/

Community Health Centers (Free/Affordable Clinics)

Community Health Centers are clinics that provide comprehensive primary and preventive care to everyone, regardless of a person’s ability to pay. Many have dental services available. Accepting some insurance, medicaid, and sliding scale self-pay. Check website for locations.

Website: https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/cdph/provdrs/health_services/svcs/find_a_communityhealthcenter.html

Period Collective (Menstrual Products)

Can assist with finding free tampons, pads, and other period products. Does not do direct donations to individuals, check their website or contact to find distribution locations near you.

Website: https://theperiodcollective.org

Diaper Bank of Chicago (Free Diapers)

Can assist with finding free diapers and baby hygiene supplies. Limited direct donations to individuals, contact to find distribution locations near you.

Website: https://www.diaperchicago.org/

Employment Assistance

These are organizations that have both job training and placement programs that intend on helping unemployed people find work. Note that some orgs have already been listed in previous parts of this document, but are repeated here for organization’s sake.

Chicago Cook Workforce Partnership (Employment Services)

CCWP is an umbrella coordinating body that oversees workforce development across both the City and Cook County. They’re functionally a triage and routing system for employment connecting job seekers with 2000+ employers in the area. Placement isn’t guaranteed but can be faster depending on work history, or they offer training to help fill skill gaps including transitional jobs as short-term employment while building skills. The Chicago Dept. of Family & Support Services uses them, but direct contact can be useful to avoid additional bureaucracy (at the loss of comprehensiveness).

Website: https://chicookworks.org/

Center on Halsted

Website: https://www.centeronhalsted.org/

Brave Space Alliance

Website: https://www.bravespacealliance.org/

Chicago Department of Family & Support Services

Website: https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/fss.html

Address: 1615 W. Chicago Ave, 5th Floor

Local / Neighborhood Organizations and Mutual Aid Groups

These are some local, neighborhood run mutual aid groups servicing communities and organising neighbor-to-neighbor connections. They can help provide food, financial aid, household items, diapers, clothes, hygiene products, and connect neighbors for sustainable community driven support systems. As community networks, they also provide basic help such as rides to work, welfare checks, and fostering community connections. Some even maintain free stores or relationships to housing programs.

Logan Square Mutual Aid

Service Area: Logan Square

Website: https://logansquaremutualaid.org/

Humboldt Park Solidarity Network

Service Area: West Side

Free Store: 3519 W North Ave (Open: Monday 4-6, Wednesday 4-6, Saturday 11-2)

Website: https://www.instagram.com/hpsolidaritynetwork

West Side Mutual Aid

**Service Area: **Garfield Park, Austin, North Lawndale, Hermosa, West Loop Website: https://westsidemutualaid.org/

19th Ward Mutual Aid

Service Area: Mount Greenwood, Morgan Park, and Beverly

Website: http://19aid.com/

Albany Park Mutual Aid

Service Area: Avondale

Website: https://www.albanyparkmutualaid.com/

Edgewater Mutual Aid Network

Service Area: Edgewater

Free Store: 1020 W Bryn Mawr Ave (Open Saturdays from 12:15 p.m. - 1:30 p.m) Website: https://edgewatermutualaid.org/

Gage Park Latinx Council

Service Area: Gage Park

Website: https://www.gplxc.org/

Irving Park Mutual Aid

Service Area: Irving Park, Portage Park

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/irvingparkmutualaid/

Lincoln Square Ravenswood Solidarity Network

Service Area: Lincoln Square, Ravenswood

Website: https://www.lsrsn.org/

Mayfair Mutual Aid

Service Area: Mayfair

Website: https://www.mayfairmutualaid.com/

McKinley Park Mutual Aid

Service Area: McKinley Park

Linktree: https://linktr.ee/mckinleyparkmutualaid

Northwest Side Solidarity Network

Service Area: Northwest Side

Website: https://www.northwestsidesolidarity.com/

West Town Mutual Aid

Service Area: West Town, Ukrainian Village, Wicker Park, Bucktown

Website: https://www.westtownmutualaid.com/

Pilsen Solidarity Network

Service Area: Pilsen

Instagram: http://instagram.com/pilsenfreestore

Rogers Park Free Store

Service Area: Rogers Park, Edgewater, Uptown

Linktree: https://linktr.ee/rp_free_store

Rogers Park Food Not Bombs

Service Area: North Side

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/rogersparkfoodnotbombs/
